fields.System.Title: Implement football_extra_bronze ingestion and validation path
fields.System.Description:
# Implement football_extra_bronze ingestion and validation path

> As a data platform engineer, I want a dedicated football_extra ingestion and validation path, so that new/<CODE>.csv feeds are ingested consistently and published as validated bronze artifacts.

## Description

Implement extra family ingestion path for source files matching new/<CODE>.csv, including utf-8-sig handling to normalize BOM-prefixed headers.

Apply row-level validation for extra-family records and frame-level validation before persistence.

Write bronze parquet outputs under football_extra partitioning with stable structure and naming.

## Dependencies

- Parent epic: Story #1.
- Depends on deterministic discovery/throttling behavior from Story #2.

## Notes

Lineage evidence:
- [architecture/football-data-ingestion.md](architecture/football-data-ingestion.md)
- [temp/8c8290a/extracted_architecture__football-data-ingestion.md.json](temp/8c8290a/extracted_architecture__football-data-ingestion.md.json)
- [temp/8c8290a/arch_extracted_architecture__football-data-ingestion.md.json](temp/8c8290a/arch_extracted_architecture__football-data-ingestion.md.json)
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Given a new/<CODE>.csv input, when ingestion runs, then the pipeline reads it using utf-8-sig compatible handling and produces normalized records.
- [ ] Given ingested records, when row-level validation executes, then required fields defined by the extra schema are enforced and invalid rows are surfaced deterministically.
- [ ] Given a validated frame, when frame-level validation executes, then frame integrity checks pass before artifact write.
- [ ] Given a successful run, when artifacts are written, then bronze parquet outputs are persisted under football_extra partitioning with consistent structure and naming across runs.
